free_text: Absolute alpha-particle induced gamma-ray yields for
Ti-Zn.
The targets in the form of 1-3 mm thick plates were
used.
. The different elemental analysis methods.
Gamma-ray spectrum is reported.
The calculated Coulomb excitation yields, reported in
table 2 are systematically close to the present results
and in most cases, as could be expected, the
experimental yield values are slightly higher.
The gamma-rays were detected with a 160 cm**3
Canberra HPGE detector.
The 5 MV tandem accelerator EGP-10-II.
The beam currents varied from 0.1 to 120 nA
depending on the ion energy and target. The total
accumulated charge per measurement varied from 0.22
to 220 muC.
Detector efficiency correction, the dead-time
correction.
.The absolute uncertainty in the gamma-ray
yield values can be estimated to be 30%.Among the main
error sources-charge integration, peak area
determination, absolute efficiency of the detector-
the inaccuracy of measurement of the incoming charge
integration is most dominating.This uncertainty
estimation was finally based on the standard deviations
obtained from the results of the two measurement series
at 10 MeV.
